description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, purple gabled roof with multiple dormer windows and a prominent central chimney. The exterior walls combine exposed timber framing with stonework on the lower level, giving it a medieval, half-timbered appearance. Blue-paned windows with lattice designs are distributed across all sides, some set within decorative wooden frames. The front facade includes a stone staircase leading to an arched entryway, partially sheltered by an overhanging roof section. Architectural details such as wooden braces, patterned shingles, and ornamental trim add to its historic charm. The structure appears multi-storied, with a complex roofline and a mix of stone and wood construction.
description: A multi-story, medieval-style house with a steep purple gabled roof, blue lattice windows, and a mix of stone and timber framing. Features include a stone staircase, prominent chimney, and ornate wooden details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a stone staircase, arched door, and the central gable with blue windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tall chimney, dormer windows, and the mix of stone and timber framing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying symmetrical timber framing, multiple blue windows, and the full height of the gabled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible dormer windows, stone base, and the continuation of the timber and stone construction.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with small dormers and grouped lower windows
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone entry with steps, partially covered by an overhanging roof and accented with timber framing
